title: Whitefly DNA Extraction and mtCO1 Gene Amplification
author: Joachim Nwezeobi
license_note: Confirmed via CrossRef DOI metadata: dx.doi.org/10.17504/protocols.io.bdvri656
materials: The GeneJETPCR purification kit (Thermo Scientific)was used to purify amplicons of interest as described by the kit manufacturer.

steps.title: Single whitefly adults were randomly chosen...
steps.instruction: Single whitefly adults were randomly chosen from each of the 1.5 ml Eppendorf tubes containing 70% ethanol, using a sterilized entomological pin.

steps.title: Each whitefly was transferred to a clean 1
steps.instruction: Each whitefly was transferred to a clean 1.5 ml Eppendorf tube.

steps.title: To extract insect DNA, 50 µl...
steps.instruction: To extract insect DNA, 50 µl of 10% Chelex was added to the tube and the whitefly was crushed using a plastic pestle until a clear homogenised mixture was obtained.

steps.title: The mixture was then incubated at...
steps.instruction: The mixture was then incubated at 56°C for 20 minutes and further incubated at 100°C for five minutes.

steps.title: The mixture was then centrifuged at...
steps.instruction: The mixture was then centrifuged at 13,500 g for five minutes using the Eppendorf Centrifuge 5424 R and the supernatant was collected and stored at -20°C until use.

steps.title: Two sets of primers, with different...
steps.instruction: Two sets of primers, with different degrees of sensitivity, were used to amplify the mtCO1 fragments, which were the: (i) African specific 2195Bt (5’-TGRTTTTTTGGTCATCCRGAAGT-3’) and C012/Bt-sh2 (5’-TTTACTGCACTTTCTGCC-3’) primers [21] and (ii) generic CI-J-2195 (5’-TTGATTTTTTGGTCATCCAGAAGT -3’) and TL2-N-3014 (TCCAATGCACTAATCTGCCATATTA -3’) primers.

steps.title: Polymerase chain reaction (PCR) was run...
steps.instruction: Polymerase chain reaction (PCR) was run on an Applied Biosystems 2720 thermal cycler for 35 cycles.

steps.title: The PCR program conditions were set...
steps.instruction: The PCR program conditions were set at (i) initial denaturation at 94°C for 2 minutes, (ii) denaturation at 94°C for 20 seconds, (iii) annealing at 52°C for 30 seconds, (iv) extension at 72°C for one minute and (v) final extension at 72°C for five minutes.

steps.title: Agarose gel electrophoresis (1% w/v in 0
steps.instruction: Agarose gel electrophoresis (1% w/v in 0.5 X Tris/borate/EDTA buffer) was used to confirm PCR amplification.
